title: Microsoft inks $9.7B deal with Australia's IREN for AI cloud capacity
text:
Microsoft has secured a five-year contract valued at $9.7 billion with IREN, an Australian company, to expand its artificial intelligence computing resources. The agreement provides access to infrastructure equipped with Nvidia's GB300 GPUs, rolling out in phases through 2026 at IREN's Childress, Texas location, which will eventually support 750 megawatts of power capacity.

Separately, IREN is procuring approximately $5.8 billion in GPUs and equipment from Dell to support this expansion.

This transaction represents Microsoft's continued aggressive strategy to address surging customer demand for AI capabilities. The company recently deployed its first large-scale cluster with Nvidia GB300 NVL72 for Azure, optimized for advanced reasoning and multimodal AI applications.

IREN, which transitioned from cryptocurrency mining to AI infrastructure, stands to benefit substantially. The firm's CEO stated the Microsoft partnership would consume roughly 10% of total capacity while generating approximately $1.94 billion in annual revenue.
